title: Hard Palate
body_html:
The hard palate describes the anterior bony section of the roof of the mouth. It also serves as the floor of the nasal cavities above.

Anatomically It is continuous with the soft palate behind it (i.e. the soft palate is the posterior border) and bordered anterolaterally by the maxillary teeth. It is comprised of the following:

  • Palatine processes of maxilla
  • Horizontal plates of palatine bones

Image illustrating lateral view of hard palate in green

Image illustrating view of hard palate looking into oral cavity

Image illustrating arterial supply and innervation of hard palate

Anatomical componentStructure
Arterial supplygreater palatine artery
Innervationgreater palatine nerve
nasopalatine nerve
Venouus drainagePterygoid and pharyngeal venous plexus*
Lymph drainageUpper deep cervical lymph nodes
Retropharyngeal lymph nodes

*venous drainage is not required knowledge for the exam
